title: Wave 0 durable command contract artifact
description: Create the shared command/receipt/read-model/outbox contract artifact before Wave 1 streams.
notes: Created Wave 0 durable command contract artifact at src/lib/provider/orchestration-command-contracts.ts. It pins receipt statuses, durable transaction row ownership, fingerprint field names, and shared TypeScript types for receipt writes, outbox requests, fingerprints, and commit plans. Validation: pnpm vitest run test/unit/provider/orchestration-command-contracts.test.ts test/unit/provider/orchestration-read-model.test.ts passed.

title: Phase 0 command read-model storage contract
description: Record durable command read-model rows, tombstone retention, and handoff policy.
design:
Command read model is command-owned, not a reuse of UI projection tables. The command transaction atomically writes durable command events, command_receipts, command read-model rows, command outbox rows, and command read-model cursor/meta. Existing UI/relay projections may lag and recover separately.

Durable rows:
- events remain the durable event boundary.
- command_receipts extends with command_type, project_key, fingerprint_hash, fingerprint_version, accepted_sequence, side_effect_sequence, result_sequence, error_code, updated_at; statuses are accepted, rejected, side_effect_requested, side_effect_completed, side_effect_failed.
- provider_command_sessions: project_key, session_id, provider_id, provider_kind, provider_session_id, status, active_turn_id, last_sequence, timestamps, tombstoned_at, tombstone_reason, retain_until.
- provider_command_turns: project_key, session_id, turn_id, command_id, status, message id pointers only, side_effect_sequence, result_sequence, error_code, timestamps, tombstone fields.
- provider_command_interactions: project_key, session_id, interaction_id, turn_id, kind, status, request/result sequence pointers, timestamps, tombstone fields.
- provider_command_tombstones: project_key, scope_kind, scope_id, optional session_id/turn_id, causation_command_id, event_sequence, reason_code, tombstoned_at, retain_until, minimal redacted details_json.
- provider_command_outbox: request_sequence primary key, command_id, project_key, session_id, provider_id, effect_type, payload_json, status, retry columns, result/error pointers, timestamps.
- provider_command_meta: project_key, last_applied_sequence, schema_version, rebuilt_at.

Tombstones retain indefinitely in Phase 0 with retain_until NULL. Current event/receipt eviction must not delete command tombstones or new command rows until explicit expired-command behavior is designed.

Expose a narrow orchestration-read-model bootstrap(projectKey): CommandReadModelSnapshot facade. It may query only command-owned tables, command_receipts, outbox, command meta, and targeted events sequence checks. It must not hydrate shell/UI snapshots or query messages/message_parts/read-query session lists. Startup recovers/rebuilds command read model if lagging and fails closed before provider lookup if recovery fails.

title: Phase 0 deterministic orchestration id and time seams
description: Decide injected clock/id service ownership and failure semantics before receipt consumption.
design: Use one orchestration-owned deterministic runtime seam. Time comes from Effect Clock.currentTimeMillis in provider command orchestration; tests use TestClock. IDs come from a provider-orchestration ID service with deterministic test implementation and live randomUUID implementation. Caller commandId remains durable identity; no fallback command IDs. Generate only artifact IDs that actually exist: turn/orchestration event/outbox IDs, not receipt IDs unless later storage proves it. Fresh command flow reads receipts first, validates provider/read-model readiness, then obtains nowMs and IDs, then consumes/commits. Duplicate reads must not advance clock or ID services. Missing/invalid commandId, ID failure, or clock failure fail before receipt writes, provider lookup, or side effects with typed orchestration errors. DB failure after ID allocation discards IDs because no durable boundary crossed.

title: Phase 0 effective dispatch fingerprint fields
description: Decide effective dispatch identity fields including provider instance, selected model, normalized options/defaults, runtime/interaction mode, settings-backed modes, and cwd/worktree when material.
design: Fingerprint policy uses fingerprintVersion 1 over a canonical effective provider dispatch object. Include commandType, sessionId, providerId, stable providerInstanceId, runtimeMode, interactionMode, normalized workspaceRoot, final prompt text after attachment expansion, ordered image digests, effective provider/model/API model actually sent, sorted material option pairs, and material resolved defaults with explicit sentinels. Exclude commandId from hash body, originId, clientId, projectSlug, rate-limit data, turnId, receipt ids/timestamps, event sequence ids, eventSink, AbortSignal, callbacks, provider handles, loggers, raw history, whole providerState, raw env, raw UI option objects, drafts, and full image data. Normalization lives at provider command boundary after settings/model/workspace resolution and before receipt lookup or side effects; persistence stores version plus hash and optional redacted diagnostics. OpenCode variant is excluded until actually sent; stable providerInstanceId must be carried before enforcement.

title: Phase 0 crash-window recovery policies
description: Decide crash-after-accepted and crash-after-provider-completion policies before side-effect reactor work.
design: Crash-window recovery policy is fail-closed. Durable event-log completion is authoritative; Conduit must not silently replay provider side effects after restart. Crash after accepted before provider side effect recovers as orphaned/interrupted; duplicate/reconnect returns non-terminal command status with result null and no provider call. Crash after provider completion before completion events/receipt update persists recovers as incomplete side-effect state with no resend; explicit retry/reconciliation is later work. If completion events persisted but receipt did not, bootstrap repairs receipt/read-model from committed completionEventSequence. If receipt says completed but event log has no completion event, treat as corruption/recovery-required and do not call provider. send_turn is non-retryable by default unless an explicit durable retry policy proves no provider attempt started.

title: Phase 0 stable provider command id source
description: Decide the durable provider command id source and prove send_turn/interrupt_turn carry it to orchestration before provider lookup.
design:
Decision: durable provider command identity comes from a new per-command commandId supplied by the caller for browser-origin mutating commands. originId remains browser/client identity for echo suppression, rate limiting, and UI attribution; it is not command identity. The relay's existing clientId:sequence command id is process-local and must not be used as durable provider command identity unless a future relay path persists that generated id and its receipt before provider lookup or provider side effects.

Propagation policy:
- SendMessage and CancelSession RPC payloads require commandId: NonEmptyString.
- The frontend generates commandId once per user intent before the optimistic send/stop action, and retries/reconnect reuses the same id until command ack or terminal failure.
- sendMessageToSession and cancelSessionById require commandId and pass it unchanged to ProviderTurnService.
- ProviderTurnServiceSendInput and ProviderTurnServiceInterruptInput require commandId.
- ProviderTurnService includes commandId on OrchestrationEngine.dispatchEffect({ type: "send_turn", ... }) and dispatchEffect({ type: "interrupt_turn", ... }).
- Orchestration rejects mutating provider commands without commandId before provider lookup, session binding mutation, receipt consumption, or provider side effects. Phase 0 proves this for send_turn and interrupt_turn; later mutating command families follow the same rule.
- Compatibility callers that cannot supply a client command id must either fail before provider dispatch or use a persisted relay-generated id committed before dispatch. No ephemeral fallback is allowed.
close_reason: Stable provider command id source recorded and reviewed

title: Phase 0 duplicate accepted return shape
description: Decide compatibility facade return shape for same-process duplicates, restart duplicates with prior TurnResult, and restart duplicates with only ack/read-model state.
notes: Decision recorded and reviewed: TurnResult remains provider completion only; durable command facade returns SendTurnCommandOutcome with turn_result, command_status, and terminal_error variants; restart completion requires completionEventSequence; accepted cannot be mapped to a synthetic TurnResult.

title: Phase 0 receipt state model
description: Decide the durable receipt states for command acknowledgement, rejection, side-effect request/completion/failure, using focused fake-provider and SQLite proof where useful.
notes: Decision recorded and reviewed: receipt states are accepted, rejected, side_effect_requested, side_effect_completed, side_effect_failed; accepted is non-terminal; legal path is new to rejected or accepted to side_effect_requested to completed/failed; side_effect_requested stores request/outbox pointer; side_effect_completed stores event/read-model pointers.

Execute sequential Phase 0 gates first, then use disjoint parallel streams only after the plan gates are green.
